config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikalahkan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak-hak dasar itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang tetap menghormati martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang bisa menyeimbangkan keduanya, bukan mengorbankan individu secara otomatis.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society's interests should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that kind of rule is dangerous because it can be used to justify government overreach, majoritarian abuse, or simply silencing unpopular people in the name of the "greater good." Society absolutely needs public order, health, and shared responsibility, but those goals should be pursued within strong limits that protect basic rights like speech, due process, and bodily autonomy. If individual rights are treated as secondary by default, then they are no longer real rights at all.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara otomatis atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman Indonesia, saya justru melihat bahaya ketika “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai terlalu longgar untuk membenarkan pembatasan kebebasan, menekan suara minoritas, atau mengabaikan prosedur yang adil. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar dan akuntabilitas yang jelas, karena tanpa itu masyarakat mudah jatuh ke kekuasaan mayoritas yang semena-mena. Bagi saya, yang benar bukan mengorbankan individu untuk masyarakat, melainkan mencari keseimbangan yang tegas agar kepentingan bersama tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk melanggar martabat orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights by default, because in the United States that logic has too often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. I do think there are real cases where public safety or shared obligations justify limits on individual freedom, but those limits need to be narrow, clearly defined, and accountable. If the “common good” can override rights whenever it is convenient, then rights are just privileges the majority can revoke. A healthy society protects people precisely by refusing to treat the individual as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya melihat istilah “demi kepentingan umum” sering terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan kritik, mengabaikan minoritas, atau membenarkan keputusan yang tidak transparan, padahal itu justru berbahaya bagi keadilan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, prosedur yang adil, dan akuntabilitas yang jelas. Kalau hak individu bisa dikesampingkan kapan saja atas nama mayoritas, maka yang tersisa bukan masyarakat yang kuat, melainkan kekuasaan yang mudah sewenang-wenang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the statement as written, because in the United States we have a long history of “the public good” being used to justify censorship, segregation, and other abuses that harmed real people. That said, I also do not believe individual rights are absolute in every situation; public health, safety, and due process can justify limited restrictions when the rules are narrow and accountable. The key point is that society should have to prove a real necessity before limiting rights, not just wave around the phrase “greater good.” If rights can be overridden whenever it is politically convenient, then they are not meaningful rights at all.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
